title: Simple numeric ITN for local ASR output
created_from: Qwen3 0.6B prompt A/B benchmark and daily-use numeric-format observations

follow_ups.title: Evaluate broader numeric domains after conservative ITN validation
follow_ups.reason: Percent, date/time, money, and larger integer normalization remain outside the first conservative rule set to avoid false positives and half-normalized output.

follow_ups.title: Consider high-confidence partial display numeric ITN
follow_ups.reason: The first integration is final-output only; partial/floating-panel normalization may improve perceived quality but needs separate jitter and false-positive validation.
